title: Fix stat allocation, unlock abilities at level 1, show class on character select

requirements.text: Fix the level-up stat point allocation so that points spent on STR (and other stats) in the LevelUpModal are correctly persisted and reflected in the Character tab's displayed stats. After spending a stat point, the Character Sheet must show the updated stat value immediately.

quotes: Level up stat points still aren't being added to the characters strength when checking in the Character tab.

acceptanceCriteria: Spending a stat point on STR during level-up increases the STR value shown in the Character tab by the correct amount.
All other stats (DEX, INT, VIT) also reflect invested points correctly in the Character tab.
The updated stat values persist across navigation between screens.

requirements.text: Fix the ability selection UI so that when a character has at least one unspent ability point (including at level 1), the available class abilities in the AbilitySelectModal are enabled and interactive, not grayed out/disabled.

quotes: I have an ability point to spend at level 1 but all of the abilities are grayed out so I can't select one.

acceptanceCriteria: At level 1 with 1 unspent ability point, all eligible class abilities are displayed as selectable (not grayed out).
Clicking an ability at level 1 successfully selects and equips it.
Abilities are only grayed out when the player has zero unspent ability points or has already filled all ability slots.

requirements.text: Display each character's class (e.g. Warrior, Rogue, Mage) on the CharacterSelectScreen character list entries, shown as a visible label or badge beneath or alongside the character's name.

quotes: On the Character select screen can we display the class of each character?

acceptanceCriteria: Each character card/row on the Character Select screen shows the character's class name.
The class label is visually distinct and readable against the card background.
All three classes (Warrior, Rogue, Mage) display their correct class name.

constraints: Do not modify files listed under frontend.immutablePaths.
Keep all backend logic within the single main actor in backend/main.mo.
nonGoals: Adding new abilities or rebalancing existing ability stats.
Changes to the dungeon combat engine or loot system.
Any new screens or navigation flows.
